meta.title: cogn, sci

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: cogn, sci
root.mentor_note: cogn and sci both mean know, and one of them, sci, went on to name science itself.

attempt.direction_prompt: Failing to notice something, or clearly recognising it?

context_sentences: She barely recognized the town after fifteen years away, every landmark changed or gone.
The board finally agreed to recognize the union after months of refusing to sit at the same table.
The patient was conscious within minutes of the surgery ending, asking for water almost immediately.
He made a conscious effort to speak more slowly during the interview than he normally would.
Charging that much for a basic repair struck the inspector as simply unconscionable.
The judge called the delay unconscionable, given how long the family had already waited.

construct_options.text: Not right or reasonable; unacceptable by any fair standard
Fully aware, awake, and responding to one’s surroundings
Completely unaware of one’s own actions or surroundings

context_sentences: The museum’s new wing was built entirely to make science feel approachable to young children.
Whatever the debate, the underlying science on the question had been settled for decades.

construct_options.text: Knowledge built through systematic observation and experiment
A vague feeling or hunch, without any evidence behind it
A failure to recognise or acknowledge something
